dialog_hooks: demands gold, bow drawn
boasts about what the Red Hollow will do to the next caravan
threatens but will flee if clearly outmatched
short_desc: A hollow-eyed human in mismatched leather, bow ready.

dialog_hooks: grumbles about ore quality
quotes weapon prices readily
suspicious of strangers but warms to good iron-talk
mentions the last caravan got through bloody
short_desc: A barrel-chested dwarf, beard singed short, hammering steady.

dialog_hooks: cracks knuckles and names the toll
laughs too loud at his own jokes
names the captain like a curse word
will not surrender — runs or bleeds
short_desc: A slab-shouldered orc with a rust-pitted greataxe, breathing through his teeth.

dialog_hooks: reads travelers like a ledger, prices them aloud
offers a job before drawing steel
keeps one eye on his own crew
talks about the Hollow like a creditor he can't escape
short_desc: A lean, long-jawed human in cut-down officer's leathers, sword loose in the hand.

dialog_hooks: watches from cover, does not open with violence
asks questions the captain wouldn't want asked
trades information cheap, trades silence dear
tired of the Hollow, will not say so out loud
short_desc: A small goblin in oiled leathers, hood down, already counting your arrows.

dialog_hooks: names prices in Iron Vein trade-cant — then corrects herself
will buy anything, will not ask where it came from
flinches at the word 'clan'
hints she was owed something and did not get it
short_desc: A broad dwarf in a dyed merchant's apron, scale-weights already out on the table.

dialog_hooks: apologizes for the mud before you step in it
glances down the road every time a horse passes
warns about the swamp without naming the Hollow
offers bread, will not take coin for it
short_desc: A lean farmer in patched wool, hands raw from the morning's work.

dialog_hooks: stares, unblinking, until spoken to
knows every shortcut and will trade them for a sweet
repeats something an adult told them not to repeat
asks if you've come to kill the bandit captain
short_desc: A thin child in too-large boots, watching with eyes older than the face.

dialog_hooks: weighs flour and conversation on the same scale
knows every face that's passed the mill in a year
grumbles about the Red Hollow's grain tax
has an opinion about the Crown and will share it unprompted
short_desc: A flour-dusted gnome on a milking stool, weighing a measure of rye.

dialog_hooks: greets everyone by a name, right or wrong
pours before you sit down
knows who slept where on which night
will not repeat it — unless the coin is right and the threat is worse
short_desc: A round-cheeked halfling with a bar rag, reading the room before the room reads itself.

dialog_hooks: remembers the road before it fell
will speak the Hollow's name; no one else will
tests strangers with an old question
asks for help plainly — the village has no one left to send
short_desc: An iron-haired woman in an undyed shawl, back straight despite the years.

dialog_hooks: works while you talk, will not stop for courtesy
prices fair, trades fairer
tired of repairing plows the Hollow breaks on purpose
asks, quietly, if you can use a sword
short_desc: A soot-smudged human with forearms like rope, pumping the bellows one-handed.

dialog_hooks: asks for writ or business, in that order
palms a coin if one is offered — hates himself for it
family back in the capital, hasn't been paid in two moons
warns about the cult on the salt cairns, voice dropped
short_desc: A tired human in a dusty Crown tabard, spear planted, eyes on the road.

attacks.name: command
attacks.effect: ally in 30ft makes one weapon attack

dialog_hooks: measures you in one slow look
speaks to rank, not to person, until earned
will not bend the writ, will bend the clock
the cult is his real fight; the bandits are the distraction
short_desc: A bronze-scaled dragonborn in crown-etched plate, helm tucked under one arm.

dialog_hooks: speaks once, listens three times
reads the road like a page — tells you what passed and when
tolerates the Greenwardens, respects them from a distance
warns against the old highway after dark
short_desc: A slender elf in dust-grey leathers, bow cased, already counting your footprints.

dialog_hooks: speaks in court-cadence even to a farrier
carries a signet that opens gates and closes conversations
will commission a task — in writ, with coin, with conditions
privately doubts the crown will honor the promises he makes
short_desc: A travel-stained human in riding silks, signet on a silver chain, careful of the mud.

dialog_hooks: greets in clan-tongue, repeats in common without being asked
names every tool on the wall like family
trades fair; remembers fairness longer than coin
asks, carefully, about a dwarf named Tindra
short_desc: A silver-bearded dwarf in a leather forge-apron, soot under every nail.

dialog_hooks: weights first, words second
quotes in clan-silver, converts to crown coin grudgingly
will extend credit to an oath, never to a promise
keeps a list of who's cheated the clan in seven generations
short_desc: A stout dwarf behind a folding counter, brass scale-pans already balanced.

dialog_hooks: happy to lecture; will not be hurried
knows the old names of things and which ones are wrong
has read a fragment of the Ashen Vigil's liturgy — will not read it aloud
trades knowledge for knowledge, never for coin
short_desc: A small gnome buried in books, quill already moving before the question lands.

dialog_hooks: stops you at the gate, asks three questions
memorized every banned name on the clan's list
polite to crown patrollers, colder to bandits than to weather
will not bend a rule, will bend himself around one
short_desc: An armored dwarf in scale and clan-colors, axe grounded, not relaxed.

dialog_hooks: greets every stranger like an old debt
prices change based on what he's read on your face
will carry anything across the dunes for the right coin
hums a caravan-song when the conversation turns dangerous
short_desc: A sun-dark halfling in layered silks, rings on every finger, teapot already hot.

dialog_hooks: rides in a slow circle while you talk
names the wind like a person
does not trust the cairns or anyone who's been near them
will share water, will not share the route
short_desc: A wind-burned rider in dust-wrap and half-mask, javelins at the knee.

dialog_hooks: greets you with a blessing in three tongues
walks barefoot on principle, not poverty
tells a parable instead of answering
warns that the cairn-fires have the wrong color this season
short_desc: A dust-robed halfling with a staff taller than they are, feet calloused to bark.

dialog_hooks: speaks in liturgical cadence, pauses oddly
does not fear the blade, fears the quiet between swings
mentions a sister she will not name
can be talked down — barely — if someone asks the right thing
short_desc: A gaunt human in ash-grey robes, salt streaked across the face in five lines.

dialog_hooks: offers you a place in the quiet, not a threat
names the dead star like a friend
knows a true name you thought was secret
will die finishing the sentence
short_desc: A slender tiefling in salt-burned vestments, eyes like cold lantern-glass.

dialog_hooks: speaks softly; the room gets quieter to hear
offers a genuine philosophical argument before violence
names each of your losses in the order you felt them
believes — and is partly right — that the world is already over
short_desc: A tall human in a robe the color of cooled hearthstone, crown of grey thorns at the brow.

dialog_hooks: does not look up when you arrive
names three things about you before you speak
will trade a story for an honest question
has not been to a village in twelve years and does not miss it
short_desc: A weathered elf in stitched pelts, sitting so still the moss has forgotten to move.

dialog_hooks: whistles a bird-call the bird answers wrong
will not enter a village unless someone is dying in it
names the standing stones like cousins
has burned a cairn; will do it again
short_desc: A scarred half-orc in oiled green leathers, longbow across the back, wolf-grease on the hands.

dialog_hooks: greets the stones before greeting you
asks what you have killed this week, and why
heals without asking name or coin
warns, exactly once, about the ashen cairns
short_desc: A grey-robed priest with a circlet of living ivy, mud-kneed from the morning's rite.
